name: Lemon-Scented Pound Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup butter or margarine, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. In a large bowl of electric mixer, cream together sugar and butter until light and fluffy. Beat in lemon juice, lemon rind, and vanilla.
2. Add eggs in three additions, mixing well after each addition.
3. Sift flour into the butter mixture. Mix thoroughly until no lumps remain.
4. Pour batter into a greased 10-inch fluted or plain tube pan. Bake until a skewer inserted in the center comes out clean (1 hour to 1 hour and 15 minutes). Cool on a wire rack for 15 to 20 minutes before turning the cake out of the pan.
Timesaver Tip: Cake batter can be made ahead and frozen before baking. Wrap the pan tightly with heavy-duty aluminum foil, label, and date. Freeze at 0°F up to 4 weeks. Do not thaw. To bake, remove foil and place in oven at 350°F until a skewer inserted in the center comes out clean (about 1 hour and 15 to 20 minutes). If the cake starts to get too brown, cover loosely with aluminum foil for the last 30 minutes of baking. Cool on a wire rack for 15 to 20 minutes before turning out of the pan.
